description: Fun animal-themed song with sound effects and playful rhythms
suno_tags: playful children's pop, warm female vocals, acoustic guitar, xylophone, bouncy, fun, 100 BPM
vocal_style: Friendly warm adult female voice, animated and expressive, playful tone shifts for animal sounds

tips: Include animal sounds as part of the lyrics (moo, quack, roar)
Use onomatopoeia freely for fun and engagement
Each verse can feature a different animal
Chorus should be the unifying theme about all animals
Add counting or listing for educational value

example_lyrics:
[Intro]
[Instrumental]

[Verse 1]
The cow says moo in the morning sun
The rooster crows when the day's begun
The little duck goes quack quack quack
Waddling down the muddy track

[Chorus]
All the animals, big and small
We love every single one of all
Furry, feathered, short and tall
All the animals, hear them call!

[Verse 2]
The kitty purrs upon my lap
The puppy barks and does a tap
The bunny hops across the floor
The parrot squawks and asks for more

[Chorus]
All the animals, big and small
We love every single one of all
Furry, feathered, short and tall
All the animals, hear them call!

[Bridge]
Can you moo? Can you quack?
Can you roar and then roar back?
Every sound is music too
Animals are just like me and you!

[Chorus]
All the animals, big and small
We love every single one of all
Furry, feathered, short and tall
All the animals, hear them call!

[Outro]
Moo! Quack! Roar! Meow!
[End]
do: Include at least 4-6 different animals across verses
Use animal sounds as singalong moments
Make the bridge interactive with questions kids can answer
Pair each animal with a simple action verb
Keep a bouncy, marching rhythm throughout
dont: Never use children's choir or kids singing in suno_tags
Avoid predator-prey dynamics or anything scary
Avoid sad themes like animals in cages
Don't overcrowd verses with too many animals at once
